title: Market Brief — Tuesday, May 19, 2026
summary: Compute is tight across every NVIDIA class on the Ornn OCPI utilization signal: H100 at 4% available (95.7% utilized), H200 at 8%, B200 at 12%, A100 at 10%. Advertised NeoCloud offerings tell a less extreme story — only 22% of H100 offerings are free vs rented — but the trade-based utilization is the binding signal. A100 spot jumped 20.1% day-over-day to $1.04/hr while H100 fell 16% to $2.40, suggesting demand rotation back to older silicon as H100 utilization saturates.
paragraphs: Compute is tight across every NVIDIA class on the Ornn OCPI utilization signal: H100 at 4% available (95.7% utilized), H200 at 8%, B200 at 12%, A100 at 10%. Advertised NeoCloud offerings tell a less extreme story — only 22% of H100 offerings are free vs rented — but the trade-based utilization is the binding signal. A100 spot jumped 20.1% day-over-day to $1.04/hr while H100 fell 16% to $2.40, suggesting demand rotation back to older silicon as H100 utilization saturates.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs $8.78/M on H100 and $9.28/M on H200, meaning any provider pricing output at $0.32-$0.40/M on 70B-class models is selling at a loss of roughly $8.50/M tokens. Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M output: Burning Cash. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M: Burning Cash. OpenAI's gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M output is the most aggressive loss-leader in the stack.

OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued when Claude Opus 4.6 delivers frontier capability at $25/M. B200 spot at $5.00/hr with the lowest TCO-adjusted inference cost ($4.58/M) and 12% util-avail is the undervalued compute trade — buy B200 capacity before utilization catches the other classes.
bullets: 4% util-availability on H100 (95.7% utilized on Ornn OCPI) is the tightest signal in the stack.
43 of 192 advertised H100 offerings are free — 22% on the offerings denominator, well above the util signal.
$0.18/M output on OpenAI gpt-oss-120b implies a per-token loss exceeding $8/M against TCO-adjusted H100 cost.
144.9% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable compute asset in the stack.
21.76 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$3.60/M output from Qwen3.5-397B undercuts every Western frontier model above 200B parameters.
